label: 635g Report to Congress; time for submission; contents

E, title LII, § 52002(b)129 Stat. 1767Dec. 4, 2015“The amendment made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall apply with respect to the report of the Export-Import Bank of the United States submitted to Congress under section 8 of the Export-Import Bank Act of 1945 () for the first year that begins after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”12 U.S.C. 635gDec. 4, 2015

Reports, , , provided that:Pub. L. 107–189, § 8(c)116 Stat. 701June 14, 2002“The Export-Import Bank of the United States shall include in the annual report required by section 8(a) of the Export-Import Bank Act of 1945 [] for each of fiscal years 2002 through 2006 a report on the efforts made by the Bank to carry out subparagraphs (E)(x) and (J) of section 2(b)(1) of such Act [], and on how the efforts are assisting small businesses.”12 U.S.C. 635g(a)12 U.S.C. 635(b)(1)

Financing for Renewable Energy Projects, , , provided that:Pub. L. 101–167, title V, § 534(d)103 Stat. 1231Nov. 21, 1989“(1)Of the financing provided by the Export-Import Bank that is utilized for the support of exports for the energy sector, the Bank shall seek to provide not less than 5 per centum of such financing for renewable energy projects.“(2)The Export-Import Bank shall take all appropriate steps to finance information exchanges and training whose purpose it is to help link United States producers in the renewable energy sector with assistance programs and potential foreign customers.“(3)Beginning on , the Chairman of the Export-Import Bank shall submit an annual report to the Committees on Appropriations on the Bank’s implementation of this subsection.”April 15, 1990

Modification of Reporting Requirements, , , modified the reporting requirements of this section as follows: This section was later amended by to require annual instead of semiannual reports.Pub. L. 93–646Nov. 8, 1965“From semiannual to annual submission to the Congress by the Export-Import Bank of Washington of a report concerning its operations under the Export-Import Bank Act of 1945 (; ).”59 Stat. 52912 U.S.C. 635g

children.heading: Annual submission of report
children.label: a Annual submission of report
children.text: The Export-Import Bank of the United States shall transmit to the Congress annually a complete and detailed report of its operations. Such report shall be as of the close of business on the last day of each fiscal year.

children.heading: Report on allocation of sums set aside for small business exports
children.label: b Report on allocation of sums set aside for small business exports

children.text: The Bank shall include in its annual report to the Congress a report on the allocation of the sums set aside for small business exports pursuant to .section 635(b)(1)(E) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the total number and dollar volume of loans made from the sums set aside;

children.label: B
children.text: the number and dollar volume of loans made through the consortia program under ;section 635(b)(1)(E)(vii) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: the amount of guarantees and insurance provided for small business exports;

children.label: D
children.text: the number of recipients of financing from the sums set aside who have not previously participated in the Bank’s programs;

children.label: E
children.text: the number of commitments entered into in amounts less than $500,000; and

children.label: F
children.text: any recommendations for increasing the participation of banks and other institutions in the programs authorized under .section 635(b)(1)(E) of this title
For the purpose of this subsection, the Bank’s report shall be transmitted to the Committee on Small Business of the Senate and the Committee on Small Business of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Technology to assist small businesses
children.label: c Technology to assist small businesses
children.text: The Bank shall include in its annual report to the Congress under subsection (a) of this section for each of fiscal years 2002 through 2006 a report on the efforts made by the Bank to carry out subparagraphs (E)(x) and (J) of , and on how the efforts are assisting small business concerns (as defined in ).section 635(b)(1) of this titlesection 632(a) of title 15

children.heading: Number of small business suppliers of Bank users
children.label: d Number of small business suppliers of Bank users
children.text: The Bank shall estimate on the basis of an annual survey or tabulation the number of entities that are suppliers of users of the Bank and that are small business concerns (as defined in ) located in the United States, and shall include the estimate in its annual report to the Congress under subsection (a) of this section.section 632(a) of title 15

children.heading: Outreach to certain small businesses
children.label: e Outreach to certain small businesses
children.text: The Bank shall include in its annual report to the Congress under subsection (a) of this section a description of outreach efforts made by the Bank to any socially and economically disadvantaged small business concerns (as defined in ), small business concerns (as defined in ) owned by women, and small business concerns (as defined in ) employing fewer than 100 employees.section 637(a)(4) of title 15section 632(a) of title 15section 632(a) of title 15

the extent to which the Bank has been able to use the authority provided, and has complied with the mandates contained, in , and to the extent the Bank has been unable to fully use such authority and comply with such mandates, a report on the reasons for the Bank’s inability to do so and the steps the Bank is taking to remedy such inability;section 635(b)(1)(E) of this title

the extent to which financing has been made available to small business concerns (described in subsection (e)) to enable them to participate in exports by major contractors, including through access to the supply chains of the contractors through direct or indirect funding;
the specific measures the Bank will take in the upcoming year to achieve the small business objectives of the Bank, including expanded outreach, product improvements, and related actions;

the progress made by the Bank in supporting exports by socially and economically disadvantaged small business concerns (defined in ) and small business concerns (as defined in ) owned by women, including estimates of the amounts made available to finance exports directly by such small business concerns, a comparison of these amounts with the amounts made available to all small business concerns, and a comparison of such amounts with the amounts so made available during the 2 preceding years;section 637(a)(4) of title 15section 632(a) of title 15

with respect to each type of transaction, the interest and fees charged by the Bank to exporters (including a description of fees and interest, if any, charged to small business concerns), buyers, and other applicants in connection with each financing program of the Bank, and the highest, lowest, and average fees charged by the Bank for short term insurance transactions;
the effects of the fees on the ability of the Bank to achieve the objectives of the Bank relating to small business;
the fee structure of the Bank as compared with those of foreign export credit agencies; and

children.label: A
children.text: the efforts made by the Bank to carry out subparagraphs (E)(x) and (J) of , including the total amount expended by the Bank to do so; andsection 635(b)(1) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: an analysis of the reasons therefor; and

children.label: ii
children.text: what the Bank is doing to achieve, and the date by which the Bank expects to have achieved, such compliance.

children.heading: Monitoring of default rates on bank financing; reports on default rates; safety and soundness review
children.label: g Monitoring of default rates on bank financing; reports on default rates; safety and soundness review

children.heading: Monitoring of default rates
children.label: 1 Monitoring of default rates
children.text: Not less frequently than quarterly, the Bank shall calculate the rate at which the entities to which the Bank has provided short-, medium-, or long-term financing are in default on a payment obligation under the financing, by dividing the total amount of the required payments that are overdue by the total amount of the financing involved.

children.heading: Additional calculation by type of product, by key market, and by industry sector; report to Congress
children.label: 2 Additional calculation by type of product, by key market, and by industry sector; report to Congress
i
children.text: with respect to whether the products involved are short-term loans, medium-term loans, long-term loans, insurance, medium-term guarantees, or long-term guarantees;

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to each key market involved; and

children.label: iii
children.text: with respect to each industry sector involved; and

children.label: B
children.text: submit to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate and the Committee on Financial Services of the House of Representatives a report on each such rate and any information the Bank deems relevant.

children.heading: Report on causes of default rate; plan to reduce default rate
children.label: 3 Report on causes of default rate; plan to reduce default rate
children.text: Within 45 days after a rate calculated under paragraph (1) equals or exceeds 2 percent, the Bank shall submit to the Congress a written report that explains the circumstances that have caused the default rate to be at least 2 percent, and includes a plan to reduce the default rate to less than 2 percent.

children.label: A
children.text: provide a detailed explanation of the processes and controls by which the Bank monitors and tracks outstanding loans;

children.label: B
children.text: detail specific planned actions, including a time frame for completing the actions, to reduce the default rate described in paragraph (1) to less than 2 percent.

children.heading: Monthly reports required while default rate is at least 2 percent
children.label: 5 Monthly reports required while default rate is at least 2 percent
children.text: For so long as the default rate calculated under paragraph (1) is at least 2 percent, the Bank shall submit monthly reports to the Congress describing the specific actions taken during such period to reduce the default rate.

children.label: i
children.text: the financial safety and soundness of the programs and funds; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the extent of loan loss reserves and capital adequacy of the programs and funds; and

children.label: i
children.text: describes the methodology and standards used to conduct the review required by subparagraph (A);

children.label: ii
children.text: sets forth the results and findings of the review, including the extent of loan loss reserves and capital adequacy of the programs and funds of the Bank; and

children.label: iii
children.text: includes recommendations regarding restoring the reserves and capital to maintain the programs and funds in a safe and sound condition.

children.heading: Categorization of purpose of loans and long-term guarantees
children.label: h Categorization of purpose of loans and long-term guarantees

children.text: “To assume commercial or political risk that exporter or private financial institutions are unwilling or unable to undertake”.
“To overcome maturity or other limitations in private sector export financing”.
“To meet competition from a foreign, officially sponsored, export credit competition”.
“Not identified”, and the reason why the purpose is not identified.

children.heading: Access to Bank products by the textile industry
children.label: i Access to Bank products by the textile industry
children.text: The Bank shall include in its annual report to the Congress under subsection (a) of this section a report on the determinations made by the Advisory Committee under in the year covered by the report.section 635a(d)(5) of this title

children.heading: Textile and apparel supply chain financing
children.label: j Textile and apparel supply chain financing
children.text: The Bank shall include in its annual report to the Congress under subsection (a) of this section a description of the success of the Bank in providing effective and reasonably priced financing to the United States textile and apparel industry for exports of goods manufactured in the United States that are used as components in global textile and apparel supply chains in the year covered by the report, and steps the Bank has taken to increase the use of Bank products by such firms.

children.heading: Report on programs for small- and medium-sized businesses
children.label: k Report on programs for small- and medium-sized businesses
children.text: The Bank shall include in its annual report to Congress under subsection (a) a report on the programs of the Bank for United States businesses with less than $250,000,000 in annual sales.

children.heading: Report on authorizations under the Program on China and Transformational Exports
children.label: l Report on authorizations under the Program on China and Transformational Exports
children.text: The Bank shall include in its annual report to Congress under subsection (a) a narrative and financial summary of the authorizations made under the Program on China and Transformational Exports.
